[ERROR] You did not use a tool in your previous response! Please retry with a tool use.

# Reminder: Instructions for Tool Use

Tool uses are formatted using XML-style tags. The tool name itself becomes the XML tag name. Each parameter is enclosed within its own set of tags. Here's the structure:

<actual_tool_name>
<parameter1_name>value1</parameter1_name>
<parameter2_name>value2</parameter2_name>
...
</actual_tool_name>

For example, to use the attempt_completion tool:

<attempt_completion>
<result>
I have completed the task...
</result>
</attempt_completion>

Always use the actual tool name as the XML tag name for proper parsing and execution.

# Next Steps

If you have completed the user's task, use the attempt_completion tool. 
If you require additional information from the user, use the ask_followup_question tool. 
Otherwise, if you have not completed the task and do not need additional information, then proceed with the next step of the task. 
(This is an automated message, so do not respond to it conversationally.)

platform: win32
node: v22.15.1
(time/terminals/tabs/visible files/todos omitted for stable VCR keying)

stream.text:
<attempt_completion>
<result>
The CLI in `app/cli.py` already supports switching the store via the `--store sqlite` argument and the `TXN_TODO_DB` environment variable for the SQLite path. The logic ensures that if `--store sqlite` is used and no `--sqlite-path` is provided, it falls back to the environment variable or a default path. The store is instantiated via `make_store(store_kind, sqlite_path)`.

To further ensure robustness, I would add error handling for `NotImplementedError` from `make_store` and clarify comments/help texts. Here is the updated `app/cli.py`:

```python
from __future__ import annotations
import os
import click
from .core import TodoService
from .store import make_store

def _default_sqlite_path() -> str:
    # Use TXN_TODO_DB env var if set, else default to .data/todo.sqlite3 in CWD
    return os.environ.get("TXN_TODO_DB", os.path.join(os.getcwd(), ".data", "todo.sqlite3"))

@click.group()
@click.option("--store", "store_kind", type=click.Choice(["mem", "sqlite"]), default="mem", help="Select store backend: mem or sqlite")
@click.option("--sqlite-path", type=click.Path(dir_okay=False, writable=True), default=None, help="Path to SQLite DB file")
@click.pass_context
def main(ctx: click.Context, store_kind: str, sqlite_path: str | None):
    # If using sqlite and no path is provided, use env var or default path
    if store_kind == "sqlite" and sqlite_path is None:
        sqlite_path = _default_sqlite_path()
    try:
        store = make_store(store_kind, sqlite_path)
    except NotImplementedError as e:
        raise click.ClickException(f"Store '{store_kind}' is not implemented: {e}")
    ctx.obj = TodoService(store)

@main.command()
@click.argument("title")
@click.pass_obj
def add(svc: TodoService, title: str):
    """Add a new todo item."""
    t = svc.add(title)
    click.echo(f"{t.id}\t{t.title}\t{int(t.done)}")

@main.command("list")
@click.pass_obj
def _list(svc: TodoService):
    """List all todo items."""
    for t in svc.list():
        click.echo(f"{t.id}\t{t.title}\t{int(t.done)}")

@main.command()
@click.argument("todo_id")
@click.pass_obj
def done(svc: TodoService, todo_id: str):
    """Mark a todo item as done."""
    t = svc.mark_done(todo_id)
    click.echo(f"{t.id}\t{t.title}\t{int(t.done)}")

@main.command()
@click.argument("todo_id")
@click.pass_obj
def delete(svc: TodoService, todo_id: str):
    """Delete a todo item."""
    svc.delete(todo_id)
    click.echo("OK")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

This ensures the CLI is robust, user-friendly, and meets the requirements for store switching and environment variable support.
</result>
</attempt_completion>
